description: Stacked area chart showing the composition of North Carolina test results by achievement level from 2021 to 2025 — a part-to-whole shown over time. The four bands (Not proficient, then Level 3, Level 4, Level 5) sum to 100 percent each year. The not-proficient band (rust, on the baseline) shrinks from 54.6 percent in 2021 to 45.0 percent in 2025, while the three proficient levels above it (Level 3, 4, 5, in a pale-to-dark teal sequence) grow — the post-COVID recovery seen as a shift in composition rather than a single line. The 2025 share of each band is labeled at the right.

title.text: The below-proficient share fell from 55% to 45% since 2021
subtitle: Composition of NC test results by achievement level (% of all results), 2021–2025 — a part-to-whole over time.
Grade-level proficient (GLP) = Level 3 and above; the rust-to-teal split marks that threshold.

usermeta.source: Source: NCDPI accountability test scores (achievement-level distribution), all subjects/grades, 2021–2025. Style-guide demo.
